message:
fcp-mesh/tests: extend harness + integration tests to cover signed-summary and signed-push paths

Further test-side migration work for the gossip signature-verification
rollout. Both `mesh_integration.rs` (scenario tests) and
`multi_node_harness.rs` (cross-node harness) were still using
unsigned summary / unsigned-push construction in additional spots
that the earlier commits didn't cover.

crates/fcp-mesh/tests/mesh_integration.rs:

* Extend the `real_component_integration` module's imports to pull
  in `fcp_crypto::Ed25519SigningKey` and `fcp_mesh::gossip::{GossipConfig,
  GossipSummary}` so the new helpers can operate directly on
  gossip types.
* New `sign_summary` helper (and peer-signing-key registration
  wiring) that accepts a plain `GossipSummary` + signing key and
  returns the signed form — a shared builder used by multiple
  tests further down in the module to avoid repeating the
  sign-then-attach boilerplate.
* Follow-on test bodies updated to use `sign_summary(...)` and to
  register the peer's verifying key on the node under test before
  dispatching through `handle_gossip_message`.

crates/fcp-mesh/tests/multi_node_harness.rs:

* Additional NodeCommand / inbound-message handlers beyond the
  ones touched previously (summary forwarding, revocation-push
  forwarding) are now routed through `MeshNode::handle_gossip_message`
  with `.expect("harness summary/push should verify")` so the
  integrity contract is enforced end-to-end across the harness.
* Local helpers for composing `NodeSignature` from an identity's
  `signing_key.sign(&summary.signing_bytes())` are reused across
  the new call sites, keeping the signing pattern consistent
  with the one added in the earlier commit.

Test-only changes — no production-code diff.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
